title: Issue when attempting to push elements into the columns
author: Quantumplation
body:
Hello,

When generating columns for a worksheet dynamically, and attempting the following code:
```javascript
fields.forEach(f => {
  sheet.columns.push({ header: f.name, key: f.name.toLowerCase() })
})
```

I receive the error `TypeError: Cannot read property 'push' of null`.  This turns out to be because `columns` is backed by a getter and a setter, and the value returned by `columns` getter has no push method.

This may or may not be intentional, but is certainly misleading when it behaves as an array in every other respect.

comments.author: kopkaa
comments.body:
I have same problem, is it fixed ??

EDIT: solution here: https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/161#issuecomment-576340460

comments.author: Quantumplation
comments.body: (That's more of a workaround than a solution, i'd still love to see API compatibility in this library)

comments.author: BraxtonLowers
comments.body:
It looks like .push silently fails when both dynamically creating columns as described by OP and when dynamically assigning data to column.values, such as
`columns.forEach((column) => {
    sheet.getColumn(column).push(data)
});
`

In the case of dynamic column creation, identifying this bug is a bit more frustrating since it only fails at 
`sheet.getColumn(column)`
with an
`Uncaught Error: Out of bounds. Invalid column letter:`
and the _columns attribute of the sheet actually does contain the correct column names. 

In any case, defining the array in advance and assigning it as recommended in [#161 (comment)](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/161#issuecomment-576340460) is an effective workaround.
